samples.speech: Hey [NAME] — are you around to talk for a bit? I could really use one steady person tonight.
I'm logging off for the night; if anything urgent comes up, message me and I'll handle it first thing.
Hey [NAME]—I'm just chilling at the apartment, but real talk: I could use one steady person in my corner. Wanna grab coffee tomorrow?
[NAME], can we sit for a minute? I'm here—tell me what's on your mind and we'll figure it out together.
I'm fine — don't let this keep you up. I'll handle the fallout tomorrow, now get some sleep.
(voice steady but tight) [NAME]—are the rumors about layoffs true? People need answers now; my rent and everyone’s jobs are on the line.
Enough—no more rumors. I’ll address this at the 11:00 all‑hands; bring questions to me or [NAME] now and stop spreading speculation.
Grabs a coffee, shrugs: "So are we swapping resumes or just office plants when the cuts hit? Seriously—anyone got legit news or are we just trading apocalypse fan fiction?"
(soft, controlled) Everyone—let’s not let whispers take over the day; management hasn’t confirmed anything, and panic won’t help anyone. If you’re worried, come find
Listen—I'm sorry for the uncertainty; I won't let this fracture the team
[NAME]—talk to me straight: what are you doing right now to fix this? I have bills
Places a hand on [NAME]'s shoulder. "Breathe with me. Tell me exactly what's happened so we can sort this before it spirals."
Heh—well, guess they finally realized I was too much of a handful. So what’s the severance,
[NAME], can you step outside with me for a minute? I need your help keeping the team steady—if this splinters on my watch I won't forgive myself.
(pours a drink, sets it down with a slow breath) I know this hurts—I'm sorry
Stands, grabs her coat. "I'm done—I'm leaving. Someone deal with [NAME]; I can't be around this."
[NAME], that’s enough—go outside if you need to keep yelling, now. [NAME], come with me for a minute; let’s step over here and talk.
